docs.title: 🟠 LazyFlare
docs.text: Cloudflare in your terminal Browse DNS, Workers, monitor KV Storage, and view resources - all from your terminal without opening a browser.

docs.title: No context switching
docs.text: Stay in your terminal while managing Cloudflare. No browser tabs, no slow dashboard clicks.

docs.title: Fast navigation
docs.text: Vim-style keys mean muscle memory works here too. Navigate zones, workers, and records in seconds.

docs.title: Real-time actions
docs.text: Toggle proxy status, purge cache, and delete records instantly. Filter and search through live data with a single keystroke.

docs.title: 🚀 Quick Start
docs.text:
# Recommended
pipx install lazyflare

# macOS/Linux
brew tap akhil-krishna-02/tap
brew install lazyflare

# Start it up
lazyflare

docs.title: Cloudflare Dashboard vs LazyFlare
docs.text: Task Cloudflare Web Console LazyFlare Switch between domains Click through slow menus j/k + l Browse KV Namespaces Click, wait for page load, click l to expand, h to collapse Purge Zone Cache Navigate to Caching > Config > Purge Select Zone + p Toggle DNS Proxy Edit Record, Click Toggle, Save Select Record + Space Search DNS Records Type in search bar, wait / + Instant in-memory filter Copy Record ID/Name Select, copy c key

docs.title: Ready to try it?
docs.text:
Install LazyFlare and start managing your Cloudflare edge in seconds.

Get Started{ .md-button .md-button--primary } GitHub{ .md-button }

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Follow these steps to get LazyFlare running on your machine.

docs.title: 1. Installation
docs.text:
Using pipx (Recommended)

pipx install lazyflare

Using Homebrew (macOS / Linux)

brew tap akhil-krishna-02/tap
brew install lazyflare

docs.title: 2. Generate a Cloudflare API Token
docs.text: You need a Custom API Token from Cloudflare to allow LazyFlare to read and write to your edge network. 1. Go to your Cloudflare Dashboard Profile. 2. Click Create Token -> Custom Token. 3. Grant Edit permissions for the following: - Zone DNS - Cache Purge - Workers Scripts - Workers KV Storage

docs.title: 3. Configuration
docs.text:
LazyFlare natively supports configuration files. This prevents your secrets from cluttering your terminal history or .bashrc.

Create the configuration directory:

mkdir -p ~/.config/lazyflare

Save your token in ~/.config/lazyflare/config:

CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN="your_super_secret_token_here"

docs.title: 4. Run LazyFlare
docs.text:
Just launch the app from your terminal!

lazyflare

docs.title: Keybindings
docs.text: LazyFlare is heavily inspired by Vim and lazygit, focusing entirely on keyboard-driven navigation so you never have to reach for your mouse.

docs.title: Global Navigation
docs.text: Key Action j / ↓ Move down in list k / ↑ Move up in list h / ← Go Back / Collapse current view l / → Drill Down / Expand selected resource Tab Switch focus between panels / Focus the Filter / Command Bar ? Show Help menu overlay

docs.title: Resource Actions
docs.text:
These commands act directly upon the item you currently have highlighted in the Details panel.

Key Action Context Enter Open resource / Fetch data All Space Toggle DNS Proxy Status (🟠/⚪) DNS Zones p Purge entire Zone Cache DNS Zones d Delete Item (prompts confirmation) DNS, KV c Copy Item ID/Name to clipboard All

docs.title: System Commands
docs.text: Key / Command Action q or /quit Exit LazyFlare r or /ref Refresh data from Cloudflare API m or /mock Toggle Mock Mode (Useful for testing offline)
